summary: Permuter-driven item matching fixes touched Link boomerang, Link hookshot, and Ness yo-yo item code. The bot report records five newly matched functions totaling +2644 matched bytes, with all five listed functions reaching 100.00%. The diff includes narrow code-shape changes such as temporary aliases, static inline helpers, declaration/order changes, scoped PAD_STACK adjustments, bool cleanup, and one argument source change from fp->x40 to attr->x40.

key_files.role: Main matching target; changed fn_802A33A0, it_802A39FC, and it_802A4758 via inline helpers, local declaration/order changes, bool control variable, PAD_STACK removal, and argument/access reshaping.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itlinkhookshot.c hunks around fn_802A33A0, it_802A39FC, it_802A4758
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_802A33A0, it_802A39FC, and it_802A4758 at 100.00%

key_files.role: Ness yo-yo matching target; split rotation update into a static inline helper and adjusted PAD_STACK placement/size.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim at 100.00%

key_files.role: Link boomerang matching target; introduced a temporary HSD_JObj alias before assigning jobj.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists it_802A0534 at 100.00%

what_changed.text: Added a local HSD_JObj* jobj_tmp alias in it_802A0534 before assigning jobj.
In fn_802A33A0, introduced static inline fn_802A33A0_GetFighter returning item->owner->user_data, reordered declarations, and changed one it_802A5E28 argument from fp->x40 to attr->x40.

In it_802A39FC, initialized item and attr at declaration, removed PAD_STACK(4), changed int var_r0 to bool chk, reordered locals, added a commented-out InitState hint, and introduced a nested y_delta temporary before assigning temp_f30.
In it_802A4758, introduced static inline it_802A4758_permuterslop returning &link_0->pos and passed that helper result to test_comp.

In itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim, moved the rotation update block into static inline itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim_UpdateRotation, changed PAD_STACK(24) inside the moved block to PAD_STACK(16), and added PAD_STACK(16) in the outer function before calling the helper.
Bot report records 5 new matches: it_802A4758, it_802A0534, fn_802A33A0, it_802A39FC, and itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim, with matched code increasing by +0.07% / +2644 bytes.

smart_moves.text: Kept edits narrowly scoped to three item translation units and five functions reported as newly matched.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report lists 5 new matches

smart_moves.text: Used tiny static inline wrappers to influence expression/access shape without changing call surface outside the touched file.
Converted the collision-check flag in it_802A39FC from int var_r0 with 0/1 assignments to bool chk with false/true assignments while retaining the same branch structure.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small helper inlines, declaration ordering, temporary aliases, and local lifetime changes can close narrow item-function matches, but they should be treated as matching tactics rather than general style unless supported by local authored-source evidence.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: New permuter yielded a few wins
raw_diff: helper inlines and local reordering
text_corpus: bot report shows 5 new matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a permuter suggests a code-shape win, keep the edit very local and rely on a narrow report for each claimed match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report names each matched function and before/after percentages

naming_conventions.text: The PR introduced helper names derived from the target function, e.g. fn_802A33A0_GetFighter and itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim_UpdateRotation.
The PR also used explicit provisional names/comments such as permuterslop and it_802A4758_permuterslop, indicating these helpers were matching tactics rather than semantically polished APIs.
Local boolean flag naming was improved from generated-looking var_r0 to chk in it_802A39FC.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce a pass-through temporary alias to preserve desired register/local lifetime shape.
examples: HSD_JObj* jobj_tmp = gobj->hsd_obj; HSD_JObj* jobj = jobj_tmp;

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Wrap a direct field/access expression in a static inline helper to influence generated code shape.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report fn_802A33A0 and it_802A4758 at 100.00%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Extract a block into a static inline helper and balance PAD_STACK placement between helper and caller.
examples: PAD_STACK(16) in helper and PAD_STACK(16) in caller

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use nested scoped temporaries to alter local lifetime or assignment shape.
examples: f32 y_delta = fp->cur_pos.y - pos.y; temp_f30 = y_delta;

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, state, author, changed files
human_pr_text: PR body says new permuter yielded wins and gives remaining item counts
raw_diff: complete diff excerpt for three changed files
text_corpus: decomp-dev[bot] report with matched-code summary
notes: There are no review comments or human reviewer discussion in the loaded slice, so reviewer intent and acceptance rationale are unavailable.
The bot report provides strong support for claimed before/after match percentages, but the slice does not include build logs, objdiff details, or adjacent regression checks.
Several introduced helpers are explicitly labeled or named permuterslop, so they should not be generalized as preferred source style without curator review and additional local-source evidence.

The diff shows a semantic-looking argument change from fp->x40 to attr->x40, but the slice does not explain whether this was a recovered original source fact, a type correction, or a codegen-driven fix.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Permuter wins may rely on narrow local code-shape steering
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2579 shows five item functions reaching 100.00% after narrow edits such as helper inlines, temporary aliases, local declaration/order changes, PAD_STACK reshaping, and scoped temporaries. Treat these as tactics requiring narrow match evidence, not as broad style guidance.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify treatment of permuter-labeled helper inlines
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding guidance that helper inlines introduced only to steer codegen, especially names/comments like permuterslop, should be narrowly justified by objdiff/checkdiff evidence and revisited for cleanup or more natural authored-source forms.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows affected functions reaching 100.00%
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already require evidence for matching tactics, but this PR provides a concrete example of permuter-labeled helper inlines that may warrant explicit curator guidance.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that permuterslop helper names are accepted naming style; the PR evidence only shows they were merged in this narrow matching context.
Do not promote the attr->x40 change as a recovered field-use rule without additional type/source evidence.
Do not claim review approval details; no review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice.
Do not claim broad regression safety; the slice includes a bot match report but no adjacent-function or full regression ledger.
